mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly reducing the present value of future corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across durable and non-durable demand channels, boosting corporate profit expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect stronger manufacturing and utility output, which correlates with higher short-term corporate cash flows and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with INDPRO levels across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 310 on the CPI index, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy, and elevated price levels erode real corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 310 across 10 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >65% of occurrences during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates robust aggregate demand and economic expansion, which lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 60-day equity returns in >50% of cases over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month decline in industrial production combined with falling real sales signals a supply-demand mismatch and inventory buildup, forcing corporate earnings revisions downward and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual decline signal yields positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances during contraction phases.
